figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Crystal structure of MnTe. The MCD is maximized when Néel vector L points along next-nearest-neighbor Mn-Mn bonds, with the sign flipping under C6z rotations. (b) Protocol for determining the Néel vector orientation θL from birefringence angle ϕ0 and the sign of MCD. (c) Optical setup for measuring MCD and birefringence by PEM-modulated reflectivity at ω and 2ω for incident polarization ϕ The incident polarization is set by a half-wave plate (HWP). (d) Normalized reflected intensity of 2.33 eV light in ω and 2ω channels, indicating clear MCD and birefringence in MnTe. (e) MCD and birefringence optical spectra compared with ab-initio DFT calculations for MnTe with L oriented along [210]. Blue vertical line indicates 2.33 eV.

FIG. 2. (a) Illustration of a MnTe sample mounted into a piezoelectric strain cell. Strain ε is applied along either the [100] or [210] directions, and optical measurements are performed at 25K. Strain ε evolution of (b) MCD, (c) ϕ0, and (d) ∆ for a sample S1 cooled under zero strain conditions. The pink band in (c) indicates that strain was applied along [210], and arrows show that strain was swept in both directions. The strain-independence of ∆ indicates that strain rotates L continuously. (e) Comparison of the effect of strain on the birefringence signal for two models: (1) detwinning, and (2) rotation of L. For (1), in general both ϕ0 and ∆ shift with strain as domain populations under the probe area change with strain. On the other hand, for (2) only ϕ0 changes. The strain evolution shown here corresponds to compressive strain applied along [210].

FIG. 3. Strain evolution of (a) MCD, (b) birefringence angle ϕ0, and (c) birefringence amplitude ∆ for a sample S2 cooled under the differential thermal strain between the strain cell and the crystal, and with strain applied along [100] (pink bands in panel (b)). Measurements are taken at two different positions on the sample p1 and p2. Strain hysteresis suggests plasticity of the magnetic subsystem. (d) Correlation between MCD/∆3/2 and Néel vector orientation θL for S1, p1, and p2. Collapse onto Eq. (1) confirms a picture where strain continuously rotates L. Least-squares fit to data yields A = 0.174. At p1, MCD is tuned to zero when L aligns with [110]/60◦. Cartoons of L and the corresponding MCD signal are depicted for p1 at various points along the hysteresis loop I → II → III → IV.

FIG. 4. Maps and histograms of (a-b) MCD, and (c-d) ϕ0 across a large free sample at 25K, showing continuously varying ϕ0. (e) ϕ0 as a function of distance around the closed loop A→B→C→A shown in (a) and (c). Vertical lines indicated error bars. (f) Collapse of MCD/∆3/2 versus θL across the free sample onto Eq. (1). Points are colored according to the birefringence amplitude ∆, and are made partially transparent to highlight high-density regions. Least-squares fit to data yields A = 0.123.
